title: HUF Spectrum Analyzer — Universal JSON Data Schema v1.0
purpose: Defines the JSON format that any domain must provide to be readable by the HUF Spectrum Analyzer. The analyzer is the fixed receiver. This schema is the tape format. Any domain that provides data in this format gets a working instrument display with zero code changes.

domain.name: string — display name (e.g., 'EMBER Electricity', 'Ramsar Wetlands Portfolio')
domain.description: string — one-line description shown in header
domain.source_type: string — what the carrier elements represent (e.g., 'Fuel types', 'Species groups', 'Sector weights')
domain.observation_cadence: string — how often data is collected (e.g., 'Annual', 'Monthly', 'Daily', 'Seasonal')
domain.data_source: string — attribution (e.g., 'EMBER CC BY 4.0', 'Ramsar Sites Information Service')

source_elements: array of strings — the carrier element names, in display order

datasets.description: Object keyed by dataset name (e.g., country, site, portfolio). Each dataset contains the time series.

each_dataset.name: string — display name
labels: array of strings — time axis labels (years, dates, periods)
complexity: array of numbers — K_eff values, one per label
velocity: array of numbers or nulls — TV distance values
peak: array of numbers or nulls — L2 drift values

carriers.status: OPTIONAL — enhances visualization but not required for core analysis
carriers.type: array of objects — full carrier state at each time point [{element: proportion, ...}, ...]
carriers.when_present: Enables Carrier Composition Ribbon (stacked bar chart showing proportional makeup over time). Each object must contain all source_elements as keys with numeric proportion values summing to 1.0.
carriers.when_absent: Analyzer operates normally on complexity/velocity/peak traces. Carrier Composition Ribbon panel is hidden. No degradation of core metrics.
carriers.recommendation: Include carriers when source data is available. Omit when only pre-computed metrics exist. The analyzer adapts to either case.
carriers.validation: If present, each carrier snapshot must: (a) contain every source_element, (b) have all values >= 0, (c) sum to 1.0 ± 0.01 tolerance. Snapshots failing validation are displayed with a calibration warning.
carriers.resolves: ISSUE-004 (Ramsar carrier snapshot inconsistency) — Doñana having metrics-only while Kakadu has full carriers is valid schema usage, not an error.

shocks.label: string — time axis label when shock occurred

drift_velocity: array of labels where velocity-based drift was detected
drift_peak: array of labels where peak-based drift was detected
each_dataset.narrative: string — domain narrative for this dataset (optional, shown in narrative panel)

Site 1.name: Site 1
Site 1.narrative: Complexity declined steadily while velocity stayed calm until the 2024 spike.
